author: codex-agent

patch.value: Add telemetry runtime diagnostics to pm health
pm health currently omits telemetry runtime/export state even though telemetry operations docs expect visibility for endpoint policy and queue/export readiness.
pm health reports telemetry enabled policy, endpoint target, queue status summary, and environment overrides without leaking sensitive values.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate check evidence ran pm context limit 10 plus pm search health telemetry check limit 20 plus pm list-open limit 20 plus pm list-in-progress limit 20 and found no open item dedicated to telemetry check visibility in pm health

message: Create follow-up issue from 2026-04-26 telemetry audit

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Follow-up evidence from latest audit: pm health --check-only --json remains blind to telemetry runtime/export queue state while remote stack is active and ingesting. Validation still needed to expose telemetry-enabled status, endpoint target, and export health summary in pm health output.

author: codex-agent
message: Backfill estimate metadata for telemetry health diagnostics

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Audit follow-up 2026-04-26: remote Prometheus and Grafana confirm telemetry ingest activity, but pm health --check-only --json still omits telemetry runtime/export diagnostics. Recommend adding telemetry policy+endpoint snapshot (sanitized), queue depth/unacked indicators, and worker insert error counters so operators can detect remote ingestion degradation from CLI health output.

author: codex-agent
message: Audit evidence logged; pending implementation scheduling

value.author: cursor-agent
value.text: Fresh telemetry health audit evidence 2026-04-26: local telemetry default-enabled commands flushed successfully to https://pm-cli.unbrained.dev/v1/events; failed exports were queued with retry metadata and redacted payloads; opt-out stopped subsequent event writes. pm health --check-only remains focused on tracker health and does not expose telemetry endpoint/queue/export state, so this diagnostics gap remains open.

value.author: cursor-agent
value.text: Telemetry analysis evidence: persisted payload key coverage includes runtime node platform data but no pm CLI version key, and start/finish deltas appeared for update, test-all, and get. pm health telemetry diagnostics should surface enough local/export state to catch these instrumentation quality gaps without raw identifiers.

value.author: audit-agent
value.text: 2026-04-26 audit confirms: pm health has NO telemetry check despite operations docs claiming it does. This is a documentation/implementation gap. Telemetry runtime health should be surfaced in pm health output.
